items.summary: A photo gallery of hightlights from World Cup 2026.

items.title: How Burger King is preparing for the GLP-1 revolution
items.summary: As weight loss drugs reshape how Americans eat, Burger King is gearing up for a future where customers still want fast food — just less of it.

items.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend
items.summary: Spain defeated Messi and Argentina to win the World Cup. The Tate brothers are arrested. And the U.S. and Iran trade strikes after two U.S. service members killed in Jordan.

items.title: Leandro Paredes se lía a golpes con Eric García
items.summary: El mediocampista argentino tuvo un tremendo cruce con el jugador español, tras perder la final de la Copa Mundial.

items.title: ¿El final de una gran historia? Lionel Messi en el pasto observa con melancolía la caída en la final
items.summary: Lionel Messi cierra la Copa Mundial con melancolía tras la caída de Argentina ante España en la gran final. Se cerró el telón para la albiceleste y queda observar a una gran generación española que se consolidó.

items.title: Usha Vance gives birth to fourth child
items.summary: Second lady Usha Vance gave birth Sunday to her fourth child, Vice President JD Vance announced on X. “We are excited to announce that our baby boy, Alec Neel Vance, was born this morning,” Vance said in a statement.

items.title: Ferrán Torres aparece al 106’ y el banquillo no contiene la emoción
items.summary: En el inicio del segundo tiempo extra, Ferrán Torres abre el marcador con un izquierdazo que termina en las redes del ‘Dibu’. El equipo técnico de ‘La Roja’ se abraza y De la Fuente busca a un Rodri que no puede contener las lágrimas

items.title: Ferran Torres anota el gol que vale un Mundial para España
items.summary: Con un hombre más en el campo, el futbolista del FC Barcelona apareció dentro del área y sacó un disparo imparable para el "Dibu" Martínez.

items.title: A cavalcade of international stars headlined the World Cup's first-ever halftime final show
items.summary: With President Trump in attendance, an all-superstar lineup that included Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Burna Boy felt a little more political than it might have otherwise.

items.title: Se calienta la final: Roja para Enzo Fernández y fuerte cruce entre Messi y Cucurella
items.summary: Sin duda, este podría ser el momento que cambie por completo el rumbo de la final, en un partido que también podría marcar la despedida de Messi de los Mundiales.

items.title: Conmovido: así entonó Scaloni el Himno de Argentina en la Final del Mundial ante España
items.summary: Se lo vio emocionado al DT campeón del mundo, oriundo de Pujato, entonando el Himno Argentino en la previa de la Final ante España, donde buscará refrendar el título junto a los suyos en Nueva York.

items.title: ¡Revívelo aquí! Justin Bieber, Shakira, Madonna y BTS brillan en el show de medio tiempo
items.summary: Justin Bieber, Madonna, Shakira, Gustavo Dudamel y BTS protagonizaron el histórico Topps Final Halftime Show, curado por Chris Martin, de Coldplay, durante la final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026 en el New York New Jersey Stadium.

items.title: Se llena de estrellas la gran final de la Copa Mundial: De Zidane a Rihanna
items.summary: La final del Mundial ha juntado a grandes estrellas que se hacen presentes en la mayor fiesta deportiva. En East Rutherford figuras del cine, música y leyendas deportivas disfrutan del duelo entre España y Argentina.

items.title: U.S. service member killed in Northern Iraq, says U.S. Central Command
items.summary: The U.S. military announced it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U.S. service member was killed in a separate incident in Iraq. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin and Keir Simmons report.

items.title: La arenga de Messi antes de saltar a la cancha: así salió el Campeón del mundo a defender el título
items.summary: La "Scaloneta" saltó firme a la cancha en Nueva York: se vio al capitán dirigir un mensaje a sus compañeros antes de salir decididos a refrendar la corona en la Copa del Mundo ante España.

items.title: Iniesta, Kempes, Alcaraz y Hoyeon presentan la Copa del Mundo
items.summary: Andrés Iniesta, autor del gol que le dio la victoria a España en 2010, presenta junto a Mario Kempes la Copa del Mundo en el Estadio Nueva York/Nueva Jersey.

items.title: ¡Se abre el telón! Argentina se prepara para el escenario de la gran final ante España en New York
items.summary: Argentina se prepara para la final de la Copa Mundial ante España. Lionel Messi y compañía se enfrenta a Lamine Yamal y la Furia Roja por el título mundial, en un show espectacular.

items.title: Juan Musso en la previa: "Nosotros seguimos siendo esos pibes que soñaban jugar un Mundial"
items.summary: El guardameta argentino destacó en la previa de la Final contra España la fortaleza de Argentina, la unión del grupo y su admiración por los compañeros, reafirmando el objetivo de defender el título en el Mundial.

items.title: Reviven el momento más doloroso de Messi a minutos de la Final del Mundial 2026
items.summary: En 2016, Lionel Messi vivió uno de los momentos más difíciles de su carrera e incluso anunció su retiro de la selección argentina. Hoy, está a 90 minutos de disputar otra final del Mundial.

items.title: Auxiliar de España dice que el equipo está tranquilo y confiado
items.summary: ‘Juanjo’ González, auxiliar técnico, tiene en cuenta que jugarán ante la actual campeona del mundo que está repleta de estrella del fútbol pero, al no cambiar la alineación inicial, dice que España está confiada y preparada para llevarse la victoria.

items.title: Ayala en la previa de Argentina: "Hoy va a salir esa parte del barrio que cada futbolista tiene"
items.summary: El asistente de Lionel Scaloni habló de cómo afrontará la "Scaloneta" la gran Final ante España y de la mentalidad del campeón del mundo para defender el título esta tarde desde Nueva York.

items.title: The soccer fans who fell in love with America during the World Cup
items.summary: The 2026 World Cup was an undeniable sensation in the United States, setting records for television ratings while millions of fans piled into stadiums to take in all the action.

items.title: US strikes Iran over troop deaths as Israel warns Tehran's attacks are coming close
items.summary: The United States has launched more airstrikes at Iran, targeting the Revolutionary Guard in response to the killing of U.S. troops

items.title: Justice Department activates untested court for 'alien terrorist' deportations
items.summary: The Justice Department has filed a first-ever petition to a secretive and dormant court created 30 years ago to weigh government requests to deport so-called “alien terrorists” from the United States

items.title: Andy Biggs and David Schweikert offer competing visions for Republican Party in Arizona primary
items.summary: Rep. Andy Biggs and Rep. David Schweikert are clashing in the Republican primary for Arizona governor

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.summary: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

items.title: Violence repeatedly erupts in dementia care despite warnings, inspections show
items.summary: Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.

items.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
items.summary: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

items.title: Deadly overnight Russian attack on Ukraine targets the capital
items.summary: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force.

items.title: 5 clever design tricks to make a small home look bigger
items.summary: Just because you live in a small place doesn't mean it has to look or feel cramped. Interior designers share creative strategies to create the illusion of space that work for renters and small budgets.

items.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
items.summary: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

items.title: MLS works to bring in a new generation of fans as the World Cup comes to an end
items.summary: In 1994, the last men's World Cup the U.S. hosted sparked soccer fever, from youth leagues to TV broadcasts to the formation of MLS. Now, can MLS harness this World Cup for a new generation of fans?

items.title: Trump celebrates the World Cup as a US victory as he prepares to present trophy to the winning team
items.summary: President Donald Trump is set to present the World Cup trophy to the winner of Sunday’s final between Argentina and Spain

items.title: Spain is the 2026 World Cup champion, defeating Argentina for its 2nd title
items.summary: Spain's run to the title was a defensive masterclass, with wins over four of the world's top ten teams. They are the first World Cup champion to win the title while only conceding a single goal.

items.title: Taylor Farms recalls lettuce shipped to 27 states over cyclospora risk
items.summary: Taylor Farms has expanded a voluntary recall of its iceberg lettuce products sourced from central Mexico because of a potential link to the multistate cyclospora outbreak that has sickened people across the U.S. The California-based company said produc...

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Florida on charges of rape and sex trafficking
items.summary: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.

items.title: Father and son arrested after holding 2 Forest Service workers at gunpoint
items.summary: A father and son are expected to face federal kidnapping charges after they zip-tied two U.S. Forest Service employees and held them at gunpoint inside a trailer in Northern California near Mt

items.title: Texas Hill Country floods test new warning systems after last year’s deadly disaster
items.summary: Officials in Texas have implemented major changes to improve flood warnings after last year's deadly floods in Hill Country

items.title: The US and Iran have blown past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
items.summary: The United States and Iran are crossing each other's red lines, one after the other

items.title: License revoked for boarding school where Paris Hilton says she was abused as teen
items.summary: Utah has revoked another license for the boarding school where Paris Hilton said she was abused as a teenager

items.title: US cancels automatic protections for imperiled animals as critics warn of extinctions
items.summary: The U.S. Interior Department has canceled a rule meant to protect plants and animals that are determined to be threatened with extinction

items.title: Paul Pelosi charged with misdemeanor hit-and-run over collision with parked car
items.summary: Paul Pelosi has been charged with a misdemeanor hit-and-run after a collision earlier this month in California

items.title: Senator says key piece of college sports legislation 'probably' has 60 votes to clear upper chamber
items.summary: A lawmaker sponsoring a key piece of federal college sports legislation says he believes the bill likely has the support it needs to make it through the upper chamber

items.title: Appeals court says 28-year sentence is too lenient for militant convicted in attack
items.summary: A federal appeals court has concluded that a 28-year prison sentence is too lenient for a Libyan militant who was convicted of terrorism-related charges in the 2012 attacks on U.S. compounds in Benghazi, Libya, that killed Ambassador Chris Stevens and ...

items.title: Federal appeals court rules that New Jersey's assault weapons ban is unconstitutional
items.summary: A federal appeals court ruled Friday that New Jersey’s bans on assault firearms and magazines that can hold more than 10 rounds are unconstitutional

items.title: A body is recovered after San Francisco boat tragedy but 2 remain missing
items.summary: Police have recovered the body of a woman who was one of three people missing after a boat sank this week in San Francisco Bay

items.title: Lawmakers demand answers after 'bombshell' report of ICE officer shooting in Maine
items.summary: Democratic members of Congress are demanding answers about Homeland Security’s vetting and training of immigration enforcement officers

items.title: Outrage after ICE agents tackle man at Las Vegas airport
items.summary: Video footage of ICE agents tackling a man at Las Vegas Airport has sparked public outrage and criticism from officials

items.title: Family to hold a vigil for man fatally shot by immigration officer in Houston
items.summary: The family of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a man fatally shot by a federal immigration agent in Houston, is holding a public vigil for him

items.title: Woman pleads no contest to abuse after 3 kids are found living alone for years
items.summary: A Michigan woman accused of leaving three children alone for years in a house of squalor has pleaded no contest to child abuse

items.title: Weather conditions again brought devastating floods to a vulnerable swath of Texas
items.summary: Severe storms have dumped over 20 inches of rain in parts of Texas, causing significant flooding

items.title: Hegseth backs low-altitude military flyovers as a series of maneuvers draws scrutiny
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is supporting low-altitude military flyovers as the maneuvers have faced scrutiny

items.title: Mississippi teachers say new state-mandated process for buying classroom supplies is more difficult
items.summary: Teachers in Mississippi say accessing state funds for classroom supplies has become more difficult with a new state-mandated process

items.title: Trump administration caps how long international students can stay in the US
items.summary: The Trump administration has finalized a rule that will prevent international students from staying in the U.S. for more than four years unless they seek and obtain government approval, a departure from long-standing practice

items.title: NYC helicopter that crashed, killed 6 last year showed signs of a bird strike: NTSB
items.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board has reported that the remains of several geese were found on the wreckage of a sightseeing helicopter that crashed into the Hudson River last year in New York City and killed 6 people

items.title: Gooooooooals: Tracking Mbappé and Messi’s battle for the all-time World Cup scoring record
items.summary: NBC News is tracking French star Kylian Mbappé and Argentine legend Lionel Messi as they battle for the all-time FIFA World Cup scoring record.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
